task: I’m trying to put together a simple plan for nights when the register gets weird — like a customer arguing over change, a card declining, or someone acting sketchy at the pump. Can you help me map out what to do first, what to watch for, and when to switch to a different approach?

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a simple **night-shift register plan** you can keep in your head.

## 1) Do this first
- **Pause and match the facts**
  - What did they say?
  - What does the register say?
  - What actually changed: money, time, pump, item, receipt, ID, approval?
- **Stay on one issue**
  - Don’t chase the whole story.
  - Focus on the exact transaction in front of you.
- **Slow the pace a little**
  - Short questions only.
  - One step at a time.

## 2) What to watch for
- **Change / cash issues**
  - Amount handed over
  - Amount due
  - Change already given
  - Whether the bill/coin counts match
- **Card decline / payment issues**
  - Decline code or message
  - Wrong card type
  - Mismatch between name, ID, and payment
  - Repeated retries with no change in result
- **Pump / fuel issues**
  - Pump number
  - Authorized amount
  - Receipt amount
  - Whether the customer’s story matches the transaction
- **Sketchy behavior**
  - Rushing you to skip steps
  - Changing the story
  - Avoiding details
  - Trying to split your attention
  - Details not lining up

## 3) When to switch approaches
Switch gears if:
- The story keeps changing
- The numbers don’t match
- They won’t answer the basic question
- They keep pushing for a fast exception
- The same problem has already been tried once and failed

When that happens:
- **Stop explaining**
- **Repeat the exact issue**
- **Use the next step only**
- **Bring someone else in if needed**

## 4) Pocket version
**Check facts. Check numbers. Check the match.**  
If it doesn’t line up, **slow down and escalate**.

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short 5-line cheat sheet** you can keep at the register.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, close enough. But I want it tighter, like actual night-shift reality, not classroom stuff.

Make it into a quick decision thing — if **card declines**, if **cash is short/change gets messy**, if **somebody’s acting off at the pump**. What I do first, then what’s the cutoff for just stopping the conversation and making them wait. Also need the part about when to keep the receipt / ID / license and when that’s enough to cover me.

Short. Like something I can glance at when it’s busy.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s the **tight version**:

## Night shift quick decision
**1) Match the facts first**
- card: decline code / amount / name
- cash: amount handed / change due / drawer count
- pump: pump # / amount / receipt
- ID/license: only if policy says to check it

**2) If it doesn’t match, stop and slow it down**
- don’t keep arguing the same point
- repeat the exact issue once
- make them wait while you re-check

**3) Cutoff for switching gears**
Switch to “hold up / next step only” if:
- the story changes
- they rush you
- the numbers don’t line up
- they keep pushing after you’ve checked it once

**4) What covers you**
- **Receipt**: keep it if there’s a dispute
- **ID/license**: keep/check only if store policy requires it
- **Card decline**: no sale until it approves
- **Short cash/change**: re-count before you hand over anything else

## Pocket line
**Check facts. If it’s off, stop talking, re-check, and make them wait.**

If you want, I can make this even shorter — like a **5-line register card**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, tighter. But you’re still kinda smoothing it out.

Give me the actual move order, like:
- card declines → what I say / when I stop
- cash short → when I re-count / when I shut it down
- pump weird → when I hold the sale / when I get someone else

And the coverage part needs to be cleaner. Like, what do I keep in my hand or under the counter so I’m not the one stuck if they start lying later. Short, blunt.

turns.agent_response:
Got you. **Short, blunt version:**

## 1) Card declines
- **Say:** “It declined. Try another card or another payment.”
- **Do first:** re-run **once** if policy allows.
- **Stop talking when:** it declines again or they start arguing the decline.
- **Hold the sale:** yes — **no approval, no sale**.
- **Coverage:** keep the **decline slip / receipt** if it prints. Note time, amount, pump/register if needed.

## 2) Cash short / change messy
- **Say:** “Let me re-count it.”
- **Do first:** count **cash in hand**, then **drawer**, then **change due**.
- **Stop talking when:** the count is off twice or they keep changing the story.
- **Hold the sale:** yes — **don’t hand out more money until the count matches**.
- **Coverage:** keep the **receipt**, your **count note**, and if policy allows, have a **second person verify**.

## 3) Pump weird / acting off
- **Say:** “Hold on — I need to verify the pump/amount.”
- **Do first:** check **pump #, amount, receipt, and approval**.
- **Stop talking when:** they can’t keep the story straight or try to rush you past the check.
- **Hold the sale:** yes — **pause it until the numbers match**.
- **Get someone else:** if they’re hovering, changing details, or giving you a bad feeling.
- **Coverage:** keep the **receipt**, pump record, and any **ID/license only if your policy requires it**.

## What to keep
- **Receipt**
- **Decline slip / transaction record**
- **Your count note**
- **ID/license only if policy says to hold/check it**

## Rule
**If the numbers don’t match, stop explaining and switch to verify mode.**  
**If they keep pushing, make them wait.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **5-line pocket card**.

user_simulator.text:
Closer. But still too neat.

I want the **actual move order** in ugly real-life version:
- card declines: what I say, what I check, when I shut it down
- cash short: what gets re-counted first, when I stop talking
- pump weird: when I hold the sale, when I call somebody else over

And the coverage part — be precise. What stays **under the counter**, what stays **in my hand**, what I’m keeping **before** I give anything back. I don’t want some vague policy talk.

Make it more like a little cheat list, not paragraphs.

turns.agent_response:
Yep. **Ugly real-life cheat list:**

## Card declines
- **Say:** “It declined. Try another card.”
- **Check:** total, card again **once**, then stop.
- **Shut it down when:** it declines again or they start arguing the decline.
- **Do not:** keep re-running it forever.
- **Keep:** decline slip / receipt if it prints.

## Cash short
- **First re-count:** the **cash they handed you**.
- **Then:** your **change / drawer count**.
- **Say:** “Hold on, I’m re-counting.”
- **Stop talking when:** the count is off twice or they keep changing the story.
- **Shut it down when:** you can’t make the numbers match.
- **Keep:** receipt, count note, second-check if you get one.

## Pump weird
- **Hold the sale when:** pump number, amount, or approval doesn’t match.
- **Say:** “I need to verify the pump.”
- **Check:** pump #, amount, receipt, approval.
- **Call somebody else over when:** they’re rushing you, hovering, or the story keeps changing.
- **Don’t release it** until the numbers line up.

## Coverage
- **In your hand:** receipt, decline slip, count note.
- **Under the counter:** anything you’re told to hold for the transaction.
- **Before you give anything back:** make a note of **time, amount, pump/register, what was said**.
- **If policy says to keep ID/license:** keep it until the issue is resolved.
- **If policy doesn’t say to keep it:** don’t hang onto it.

## One-line rule
**If the numbers don’t match, stop talking, re-check once, then wait or escalate.**

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, closer. But I want the **exact hold points** cleaner.

Like:
- card declines → what stays in my hand while I’m checking, and what line makes me stop arguing
- cash short → when I keep the bill / receipt / change in view, and when I just stop and make them wait
- pump weird → what I can hold back before anything gets handed out, and when I call somebody over

Also, the coverage part — don’t say it like a policy sheet. I want the **real pile**: what I keep right there, what I put under the counter, what I don’t give back until I’ve got my note or receipt.

Shorter if you can. Like a dirty little checklist, not a clean writeup.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — **dirty checklist version**:

## Card declines
- **In my hand:** card, receipt/decline slip, transaction screen in view
- **Hold point:** after **one re-try** if policy allows
- **Stop arguing when:** it declines again or they start looping the same story
- **Line:** “It’s declining. You’ll need another payment.”

## Cash short
- **In view:** bill, change, receipt, drawer total
- **First re-count:** what they handed you
- **Then:** your change / drawer
- **Hold point:** before you hand back anything else
- **Stop and wait when:** the count is off twice or the story changes
- **Line:** “I’m re-counting. Hold on.”

## Pump weird
- **Hold back:** the sale / final release
- **In hand:** receipt, pump number, amount, approval
- **Call somebody over when:** pump #, amount, or story doesn’t match, or they start rushing you
- **Hold point:** before anything gets finalized or handed out
- **Line:** “I need to verify this first.”

## Coverage pile
- **Right there:** receipt, decline slip, count note, pump info
- **Under the counter:** anything you’re told to keep with the transaction
- **Don’t give back yet:** until you’ve got the note/receipt and the details written down
- **Keep:** time, amount, pump/register, what was said

## Rule
**One check, one re-check, then stop talking and make them wait.**
